summary: Verified infinite loop bug fix in nxt8_langgraph_ultra.py - all 10 tests passed. The human_approval stub that caused graph to route back into itself forever has been properly removed.
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix verified: _router now returns END when requires_human_approval=True and approved=False (line 209-210)
human_approval_node function confirmed removed from module
_build_graph confirmed to have no 'human_approval' node or edges
Router only returns valid targets: END, 'hermes', or 'tools' - no infinite loop path exists
context_for_next_testing_agent: Human approval infinite loop bug is fixed and verified. The nxt8_langgraph_ultra.py module now properly terminates via END when requires_human_approval=True and approved=False. Test file test_human_approval_loop_fix.py covers all edge cases.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: The original code had a human_approval_node stub that routed back to supervisor when requires_human_approval=True and approved=False, creating an infinite loop. Fix: Router now returns END instead of routing to human_approval, and the human_approval_node/edges were removed entirely.
